Article Description: Is there a mechanism that identifies GDPR data in each system?
GDPR Best Practice: An asset inventory system is an automated approach to meet this requirement. If you don't have one, excel can be used.

Article Description: Is there a mechanism for identifying which systems are processing personal data wholly or partly by automated means?
Is there a mechanism in place for identifying systems that process personal data for people in the European Union whether is it processed in the EU or elsewhere?
N/A.

Article Name: Principles relating to personal data processing
Article Description:
Is there a mechanism in place that ensures personal data is  processed lawfully, fairly and transparently?
Is collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes only?
Is adequate, relevant and limited?
Is accurate?
Is kept no longer than needed?
Is the system is measured to ensure it is processed securely to in terms of integrity and confidentiality?

Article Name: Principles relating to personal data processing
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place that ensures personal data is  processed lawfully, fairly and transparently?

Article Name: Principles relating to personal data processing
Article Description: Is collected for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes only?

Article Name: Principles relating to personal data processing
Article Description: Is adequate, relevant and limited?

Article Name: Principles relating to personal data processing
Article Description: Is accurate?

Article Name: Principles relating to personal data processing
Article Description: Is kept no longer than needed?

Article Name: Principles relating to personal data processing
Article Description: Is the system is measured to ensure it is processed securely to in terms of integrity and confidentiality?
Is there a mechanism in place to ensure that the stated purpose is clear? And there is consent to process?
it is required by a contract?
It is this necessary for other compliance reasons?
It is necessary to protect someone's vital interests?
It is required for public interest or an official authority?
It is limited due to the subject is a child?
Is there a mechanism to verify the data subject's consent was freely given and they can withdraw it easily at any time?

Article Name: Conditions applicable to child s consent in relation to information society services
Article Description: Is there a mechanism that allows for special restrictions apply to consent by/for children?

Article Name: Processing of special categories of personal data
Article Description: Has consent been given sensitive data processing in regards to a person's race, political opinions, religion, sexuality, genetic info and other biometrics?

Article Name: Processing of data relating to criminal convictions and offences
Article Description: Is there a mechanism for specific circumstances relating to criminal convictions and offenses?

Article Name: Processing which does not require identification
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place to not know the identity of the person?

Article Name: Transparent information, communication and modalities for the exercise of the rights of the data subject
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for communications with data subjects that ensures they are transparent, clear and easily understood?

Article Name: Information to be provided where personal data are collected from the data subject
Article Description: Is there a mechanism for the provision of fair processing information in terms of if their info will be exported (especially outside the EU), how long the info will be held, their rights and how to enquire/complain etc?

Article Name: Information to be provided where personal data have not been obtained from the data subject
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for similar notification requirements to Article 13 if personal info is obtained indirectly (e.g. a commercial mailing list)?

Article Name: Right of access by the data subject
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for people to find out whether the organization holds their personal info, what it is being used for, to whom it may be disclosed etc., and be informed of the right to complain, get it corrected, insist on it being erased etc.?
Is there a mechanism in place for people to get their personal info corrected, completed, clarified etc.?

Article Name: Right to erasure ( right to be forgotten )
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for people have a right to be forgotten i.e. to have their personal info erased and no longer used?
Is there a mechanism in place for people have a right to restrict processing of their personal info?

Article Name: Notification obligation regarding rectification or erasure of personal data or restriction of processing
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for people to know the outcome of requests to have their personal info corrected, completed, erased, restricted etc.?
Is there a mechanism in place for people to obtain a usable ‘portable' electronic copy of their personal data to pass to a different controller?

Section Name: Right to object and automated individual decision making
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for people to object to their information being used for profiling and marketing purposes?

Section Name: Right to object and automated individual decision making
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for to insist that key decisions arising from automatic processing of their personal info are manually reviewed/reconsidered?
Is there a mechanism in place to identify national laws that may modify or override various rights and restrictions for national security and other purposes?

Is there a data protection officer (DPO) in place that acts as the “controller” and is responsible for implementing appropriate privacy controls (including policies and codes of conduct) considering the risks, rights and other requirements within and perhaps beyond GDPR?

Article Name: Data protection by design and by default
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for privacy by design?

Is there a mechanism in place where organizations are jointly responsible for determining and fulfilling privacy requirements collaboratively that clarifies their respective roles and responsibilities?

Article Name: Representatives of controllers not established in the Union
Article Description: For organizations outside Europe, is there a formal privacy representative? And inside Europe if they meet certain conditions (e.g. they routinely supply goods and services to, or monitor, Europeans)?
If an organisation uses one or more third parties to process personal info (‘processors'),  can it ensure they too are compliant with GDPR?

Article Name: Processing under the authority of the controller or processor
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place to ensure processors only process personal info in accordance with instructions from the controller and applicable laws?

Is there a mechanism in place for controllers to maintain documentation concerning privacy e.g. the purposes for which personal info is gathered and processed, ‘categories' of data subjects and personal data etc.?
Is there a policy in place to ensure that organizations cooperate with the authorities e.g. privacy or data protection ombudsmen?

Are there mechanism in place to ensure that organizations implement, operate and maintain appropriate technical and organizational security measures for personal info, addressing the information risks?

Article Name: Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place to notify the authorities promptly (within 3 days of becoming aware of them unless delays are justified) for privacy breaches that have exposed or harmed personal information?

Article Name: Communication of a personal data breach to the data subject
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place to notify the authorities promptly for privacy breaches that have exposed or harmed personal info and hence are likely to harm their interests ‘without undue delay'?

Section Name: Data protection impact assessment and prior consultation
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place to access privacy risks including potential impacts, particularly where new technologies/systems/arrangements are being considered, or otherwise where risks may be? Is there a mechanism to rank  ‘Significantly risky situations'?

Section Name: Data protection impact assessment and prior consultation
Article Description: Is there a mechanism to notify authorities for privacy risks assessed as “high”?

Article Name: Designation of the data protection officer
Article Description: Is there a data protection officer position?

Article Name: Position of the data protection officer
Article Description: Is the DPO supported by the organization and engaged in privacy matters?

Article Name: Tasks of the data protection officer
Article Description: Does the DPO offer advice on privacy matters, monitor compliance, liaise with the authorities, act as a contact point, address privacy risks etc.?
Is there a mechanism to align the GDPR assessment process with other codes of conduct, regulations and guidelines?

Article Name: Monitoring of approved codes of conduct
Article Description: Is there a mechanism to demonstrate the GDPR assessment process with other codes of conduct, regulations and guidelines?
N/A. Similar schemes already exist: GDPR gives them some official recognition, on top of the commercial advantages they already exploit.

N/A. This should improve the credibility and meaning of privacy seals and marks, but may also increase the costs.  Since they are voluntary, whether or not to be certified, and which schemes to join, are commercial/business matters for management.

Chapter Name: Transfer of personal data to third countries of international organizations
Article Description: Is there a mechanism for international transfers and processing of personal info to be complaint with GDPR?

Chapter Name: Transfer of personal data to third countries of international organizations
Article Name: Transfers of the basis of an adequacy decision
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place to identify which systems transfer GDPR data and to which locations that are identified as adequate?  Storm can list identified countries with adequate privacy arrangements and systems that use them. Most formalities are to be handled by the Commission. Compliance involves avoiding transfers to other countries, monitoring the official lists for changes, and ensuring that suitable contracts/agreements and other privacy controls are in place as with other third party data transfers (see Article 28 especially).

Chapter Name: Transfer of personal data to third countries of international organizations
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place to identify which systems transfer GDPR data and to which locations that are identified as not adequate?    Is there a mechanism in place for the organization to verify the implementation and adequacy of privacy controls before transferring personal data to such countries, and subsequently e.g. suitable contractual clauses and compliance activities?

Chapter Name: Transfer of personal data to third countries of international organizations
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place for national authorities to approve legally-binding privacy rules permitting transfers to non-approved countries?

Chapter Name: Transfer of personal data to third countries of international organizations
Article Name: Transfers or disclosures not authorised by union law
Article Description: N/A. Such situations would normally be handled by legal and regulatory compliance specialists - but may start out as incidents.

Chapter Name: Transfer of personal data to third countries of international organizations
Article Description: Is there a mechanism in place to measure the risk of data transfers that are to non-approved countries?

Article Description: Is there a mechanism to communicate with international authorities on privacy issues?
